reasoning: As of early 2025, 48 African Union member states have deposited their instruments of ratification for the AfCFTA. Libya has been consistently listed among the seven holdouts (Benin, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Somalia, South Sudan, and Sudan) that have signed but not yet ratified the agreement. While the Libyan Ministry of Economy and Trade has engaged in capacity-building workshops and submitted provisional tariff schedules, the country remains mired in a political stalemate between the Government of National Unity (GNU) and the House of Representatives (HoR), which significantly hinders the formal legislative process required for treaty ratification. With no evidence of any legislative breakthrough as of May 1, 2025, it is highly unlikely that Libya will complete the ratification and deposition process by the July 1, 2025 deadline.
